info.name: secure-agent
info.description:
Complete API reference for the secure-agent server.

**Auth**: Most endpoints require the `x-secret` header matching `api.secret` in `config/settings.json`.

**Variables**:
- `base_url` — HTTP transport (default: `http://127.0.0.1:3010`)
- `internal_url` — Internal send API (default: `http://127.0.0.1:3001`)
- `secret` — Value of `api.secret` from `config/settings.json`

**Folders**:
1. **Utility** — Health, capabilities, governance, approvals
2. **Customer pipeline** — Simulate WhatsApp customer messages
3. **Agent management** — Inspect and hot-edit intents and tools without restarting
4. **Admin — query** — Natural language DB queries (no agent loop)
5. **Admin — agent** — Full agentic tasks (uses agent loop + governance)
6. **Internal send API** — Push outbound WhatsApp messages

item.name: Setup auth
item.description: Login to get the session cookie required by all /setup/* endpoints (admin queries, agent tasks, profile, governance, approvals).

request.description: Returns a `secureai_session` cookie (12 h TTL). Postman stores it automatically — subsequent /setup/* requests will include it. Run this once before using any Admin or Setup endpoint.
Clears the session cookie.

item.name: Workspace management
item.description: Manage workspaces — list, switch, load/save profiles, generate agent drafts, and promote drafts to live.

request.description: Returns all workspace IDs and which one is currently active.
Switches the active workspace. All subsequent requests without an explicit workspaceId will use this one.
Returns the workspace profile (business name, DB path, tools config), draft files list, and workspace summary. Pass `?workspace=` to target a specific workspace.

body.raw:
{
  "businessName": "Ray's Home Kitchen",
  "cuisine": "home-style Indian food",
  "tone": "warm, sharp, and naturally business-aware",
  "dbPath": "/Users/deepjyotiray/Documents/FoodWebsite/ray-orders-backend/orders.db",
  "workspaceId": "default"
}

request.description: Saves the workspace profile. All fields are merged into the existing profile. Include `workspaceId` to target a specific workspace.

body.raw:
{
  "businessName": "Ray's Home Kitchen",
  "cuisine": "home-style Indian food",
  "tone": "warm, sharp, and naturally business-aware",
  "dbPath": "/Users/deepjyotiray/Documents/FoodWebsite/ray-orders-backend/orders.db",
  "workspaceId": "default"
}

request.description: Saves the profile then generates a full agent draft (manifest, FAQ, policy files). Returns the generated intent names, FAQ topics, and keyword count. Does NOT go live until you call Promote.
Copies draft files to the live agent config. The agent will reload with the new manifest, FAQ, and policy on next request.

item.name: Setup chat
item.description: Test the agent in draft or live mode. If a draft exists for the workspace, the message is routed to the draft agent. Otherwise it goes to the live agent.

request.description: Sends a test message. Response includes `source: 'draft'` or `source: 'live'` so you know which agent handled it.

item.name: Utility
item.description: Diagnostic and observability endpoints. No auth required for /health and /capabilities.

request.description: Returns `{ status, agent, timestamp }`. No auth required. Use this to confirm the server is up and an agent manifest is loaded.
Returns the agent name, list of intent names, list of tool names, and a governance summary (role, worker count, governed tool count). No auth required.
Full governance policy dump — role, all tool policies (category, risk, approval mode, mutating flag), and worker allowlists. Requires x-secret.
Lists all tool calls currently blocked and waiting for manual approval. Returns array of `{ id, tool, args, requestedAt }`. Requires x-secret.
Approves a pending tool call by its ID (from the list above). The blocked agent turn will resume. No x-secret required (setup session auth).

Merges partial updates into admin-governance.json. Pass only the keys you want to change — `tools`, `roles`, `workers`, or `defaultRole`. Example: remove observer from query_db access. Requires setup session cookie.
Restores query_db access to all roles (undo the restriction above).
Returns all agent workers (planner, researcher, operator, coder) and their allowed tool lists.

item.name: Customer pipeline
item.description: Simulate inbound WhatsApp messages from a customer. The agent runs the full pipeline: sanitize → intent route → tool execute. Use a real phone number format (e.g. 919XXXXXXXXX).

request.description: Triggers the `greet` intent → `concierge` tool. Good first test to confirm the agent is responding.
Triggers the `show_menu` intent → `rag_menu` tool. Queries the menu table in orders.db and returns formatted results.
Triggers the `order_status` intent → `order_lookup` tool. Looks up orders by phone number.
Triggers the `place_order` intent → `order_create` tool. Starts a cart session for the phone number.
Triggers the `support` intent → `support_faq` tool. Shows the support menu and escalation options.
Triggers the `general_chat` intent → `concierge` tool. Business-aware small talk and recommendations.

item.name: Agent management
item.description:
Hot-edit the live agent manifest (intents + tools) without restarting the server. Changes are written to the YAML file immediately.

**Intent** = a customer goal (e.g. `show_menu`) mapped to a tool.
**Tool** = a capability the agent can invoke (e.g. `rag_menu`, `order_lookup`).

All endpoints require x-secret.

request.description: Returns all intents with full detail: `name`, `tool`, `auth_required`, and `hint` (the routing hint shown to the LLM classifier). Use this to audit what the agent can currently handle.
Returns full detail for one intent by name. Returns 404 if not found. Change `show_menu` in the URL to any intent name.

Adds a new intent to the live manifest. `name` and `tool` are required. `tool` must already exist in the tools section. `hint` is the routing hint shown to the LLM classifier — write it as a natural language description of when this intent should fire.
Removes an intent from the live manifest by name. Returns 400 if the intent doesn't exist. Change `track_delivery` in the URL to the intent you want to remove.
Returns all tools with their full config: `name`, `type`, and all type-specific fields (db_path, system_prompt, escalation_phone, etc.). Use this to see exactly what each tool is configured with.
Returns full config for one tool by name. Returns 404 if not found. Change `rag_menu` in the URL to any tool name.
Adds a RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) tool. Queries the DB and feeds results to the LLM. Required fields: `name`, `type: rag`, `db_path`, `system_prompt`.
Adds a support tool. Shows a support menu and escalates to a human via phone. Required fields: `name`, `type: support`, `db_path`, `escalation_phone`.

Adds an order-lookup tool backed by SQLite. Looks up orders by phone, generates UPI QR codes, and shows delivery status. Required fields: `name`, `type: sqlite`, `db_path`, `upi_handle`, `website`, `brand_label`, `country_code`.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "concierge_v2",
  "type": "business_chat",
  "business_name": "Ray's Home Kitchen",
  "cuisine": "home-style Indian food",
  "tone": "warm, sharp, and naturally business-aware",
  "greeting": "Welcome to Ray's Home Kitchen.",
  "signature_line": "If you're hungry, I can also suggest something from today's menu.",
  "db_path": "/Users/deepjyotiray/Documents/FoodWebsite/ray-orders-backend/orders.db"
}

request.description: Adds a general-purpose business chat tool. Used for greetings, small talk, and out-of-domain questions. Required fields: `name`, `type: business_chat`, `business_name`, `db_path`.

Removes a tool from the live manifest by name. Returns 400 if the tool doesn't exist. Note: intents pointing to this tool will break — delete or update those intents first. Change `rag_offers` in the URL to the tool you want to remove.

item.name: Admin — query
item.description:
Natural language queries against the orders.db database. Runs in `query` mode — LLM generates SQL dynamically from the DB schema, executes it (read-only), and summarises the results.

Governance: checked against the `query_db` tool policy. Pass `role` in the body to test different access levels (defaults to super_admin).

Requires setup session cookie (run Login first).

item.name: How much did I make today

request.description: Sums today's revenue from the orders table. LLM generates SQL dynamically against the DB.
Lists all orders with payment_status != paid.
Calculates income minus expenses for the current month from the expenses table.
Lists orders placed today that are not yet delivered or cancelled.
Total revenue from all paid orders in the current calendar year.
Ranks menu items by order frequency for the current month.
Lists all expense entries from the expenses table for the current week. Dates are stored as DD/MM/YYYY.
Demonstrates governance: if observer is removed from query_db roles in admin-governance.json, this request will be blocked with a reason.

item.name: Admin — agent
item.description:
Full agentic tasks. Runs in `agent` mode — uses the admin agent loop with 35+ tools, governance, and self-healing. Use this for mutations, system tasks, and multi-step operations.

Prefix your task with `agent` or just write it naturally — the server prepends `agent` automatically in agent mode.

request.description: Runs `pm2 list` via the shell tool and returns process status. Good first test for the agent loop.
Agent queries the DB and formats a list of unpaid orders.
Mutating operation — agent updates the order status in the DB. Governed by admin-governance.json (low risk, no approval needed).
Agent uses the `add_expense` tool to insert a row into the expenses table. Date stored as DD/MM/YYYY.
Agent fetches the website and summarises what it finds — useful for checking if the site is live and what it shows.
Agent runs the shell command and returns the last 50 lines of the agent log.
Agent looks up the coupon in the coupons table and applies it to the specified order.
Sends a base64-encoded image (e.g. a grocery receipt) to the vision LLM. It extracts expense/income entries and inserts them directly into the expenses table. Returns the list of added entries.

item.name: Internal send API
item.description: Push outbound WhatsApp messages directly to a phone number. Runs on the internal port (3001) — not exposed publicly. Requires x-secret.

request.description: Sends a WhatsApp message to the given phone number via the connected WhatsApp session. Phone must include country code with + prefix.
